description:
Audit existing FlowDoc documentation for drift and apply fixes, one item per 
loop/judge cycle. Spawns: root epic -> a single audit spawner (detect drift, 
score by impact, write _UpdateQueue.md) -> one bead per queue item created at 
runtime (UPDATE / NEW / REMOVE / VERIFY / LEAKAGE) -> a finalize bead gated on 
all of them that archives the queue into _AuditLog.md. Replaces the 
flowdoc-maintainer / flowdoc-user-maintainer agent-loop. Requires the matching 
DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md already exists (run flowdoc-generate first). Run via 
'bd mol pour flowdoc-maintain --var audience=maintainer|user'.

audience.description: maintainer = audit __docs/ (engineer impact scoring); user = audit _docs/ (end-user impact scoring + developer-leakage scan).

steps.title: FlowDoc: maintain {{audience}} documentation
steps.description:
Umbrella for a FlowDoc maintenance pass ({{audience}} edition).

AUDIENCE MAP:
  - maintainer -> audit __docs/ ; score by engineer/maintainer impact.
  - user       -> audit _docs/  ; score by end-user impact; also strip any
    developer content that leaked into user docs.

Two authored children: 'audit' (detect drift + spawn one fix bead per finding)
and 'finalize' (archive the queue once every fix is closed). Work the leaves,
not the epic. Precondition: DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md must already exist.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: audit for drift, then spawn one fix bead per finding
steps.description:
AUDIT + FAN-OUT (one bead). Your bead id is shown as "Complete beads issue
<id>" at the top of this goal — call it $SELF.

AUDIENCE = {{audience}}.  DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.
PRECONDITION: if DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md does not exist, stop and report that
flowdoc-generate must be poured first — do NOT spawn anything.

== Part A: detect drift (one pass) ==
1. Survey current code: list_files(".", recursive=True); read dependency
   manifests for version changes; note new / renamed / deleted files.
2. Load doc state: read DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md and each doc's Implementation
   Map (maintainer) / documented steps (user).
3. Run detection strategies:
   - Implementation Map check: do the files a doc cites still exist and match?
   - Dependency drift: current manifest vs Architecture.md tech-stack table.
   - Entry-point check: undocumented routes/commands/exports, and documented
     entry points that no longer exist.
   - Config drift: documented config keys vs actual config/.env.
   - (user edition ONLY) Developer-leakage scan of existing docs: source paths
     (src/, *.ts, *.py), shell/install commands (npm/git/make), internal URLs
     (localhost/127.0.0.1/staging/.dev).
4. Score each finding by impact and bucket it:
   - maintainer: entry/public-API change +10, core flow +8, new dep +5,
     config +5, helper +2, cosmetic 0.
   - user: documented step broken +10, UI moved +8, new required step +8,
     feature removed-but-documented +7, new capability undocumented +5,
     flag/option changed +5, config +4, leakage +4, error-text +2, wording +1.
   Tiers: >=8 critical, 4-7 medium, 1-3 low, 0 verify-only.
5. Write DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md: audit summary, findings-by-category table,
   counters, and the queue ordered by score, each line tagged with an action:
   "- [ ] U001 | UPDATE | [Name](Section/Name) | <what drifted>".
   Append a dated entry to DOCS_DIR/_AuditLog.md.

== Part B: fan out — ONE bead per queue item (deadlock-safe contract) ==
For EACH queue item create exactly one child bead:
  bd create --type=task --priority=<1 if critical, 2 if medium, 3 if low> --parent=$SELF --title='FlowDoc {{audience}} <ACTION>: <Name>' --description='<ACTION> the doc for audience={{audience}}.
       UPDATE = re-read source, fix the drifted sections, add a > [!NOTE]
         Last Updated stamp. NEW = create the doc from the matching template in
         DOCS_DIR/_FlowDocGuide.md and add it to _Manifest.md. REMOVE = delete
         the doc, drop it from _Manifest.md and any index, fix inbound links.
         VERIFY = re-read source, confirm accurate, mark verified (no rewrite).
         LEAKAGE = replace developer content with user-facing equivalents.
       Then tick this item -> [x] in _UpdateQueue.md and bump counters.
       Acceptance: action applied, queue + manifest updated, links valid.'
WIRING RULES — obey exactly to stay deadlock-free:
  - Parent EVERY spawned bead to $SELF (so the finalize fan-out gate counts it).
  - No dependency from any fix bead to the finalize bead.
  - No blocks edges between fix beads — they are independent.
  - Do NOT create your own finalize/rollup bead; one already exists and waits.
  - Spawn AT LEAST ONE child. If there is genuinely no drift, spawn a single
    VERIFY bead ("confirm docs current, no drift found") so the gate resolves.
  - Do NOT close any bead — the LLM judges close. (bead-chain blocks bd close.)

Stop after writing the queue + spawning. Summarize findings and how many fix
beads you spawned.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: finalize maintenance (archive queue to audit log)
steps.description:
FINALIZE the {{audience}} maintenance pass. You become ready only once 'audit'
and EVERY fix bead it spawned are closed (fan-out gate).

DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.
1. Confirm every item in DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md is checked [x]; fix counters.
2. Append a completion summary (updates made / new / removed / verified) to
   DOCS_DIR/_AuditLog.md, then delete DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md.
3. Verify _Manifest.md, all index files, and intra-doc links are consistent
   after the additions/removals.
READ-ONLY source. Output only under DOCS_DIR. Do NOT close any bead yourself.
